place: Bogota borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Bogota Public Library’s Local History archive, funded by a 2019 appeal and a Bergen County grant, preserves the town’s past.
The archive spans 125 years, featuring books, newspaper clippings, atlases, and artifacts that chronicle Bogota’s evolution.
The Bogota History Collection, assembled over years by library staff, offers a curated glimpse into the borough’s heritage.
After Joseph Academy closed in 2020, its photographs and ephemera were donated to the library, forming the Joseph Academy Collection.
The official Borough of Bogota website welcomes residents and visitors with updates on local events and municipal news.
Bogota, New Jersey, is one of 253 boroughs in the state, a municipal form rooted in the 19th‑century Borough Act.
